data.text: I found the main landing implementation and a few nearby tests that already exercise merge rollback and post-land preservation. Next I’m reading the exact control flow around the failure path so I can extend it without changing unrelated land behavior.

data.output:
package agent

import (
	"errors"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestRealLandingGitOpsUpdateRefToRefusesHEAD(t *testing.T) {
	err := (RealLandingGitOps{}).UpdateRefTo(t.TempDir(), "HEAD", "deadbeef", "")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "refusing to update HEAD directly")
}

func TestMarkResultExecutionErrorReturnsStructuredReport(t *testing.T) {
	res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
		BeadID:    "ddx-error",
		AttemptID: "attempt-1",
		BaseRev:   "base",
		ResultRev: "base",
		ExitCode:  1,
		Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
	}

	MarkResultExecutionError(res, errors.New("failed to read worktree HEAD: exit status 128"))
	report := ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, "standard")

	assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, report.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, "standard", report.PowerClass)
	assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "failed to read worktree HEAD")
}

func TestMarkResultLandErrorReconcilesAlreadyLandedWorkerCommit(t *testing.T) {
	repo := initReportTestRepo(t)
	base := gitReportTest(t, repo, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(repo, "worker.txt"), []byte("worker\n"), 0o644))

data.text: I’ve confirmed the current land code already restores after post-land gate and evidence failures, but the merge-conflict path still just returns a preserved result with no explicit cleanup check. I’m now looking at the existing land tests to reuse the real-git fixture and make the new assertions line up with the current semantics.

The core decision point is the post-ref-update checkout sync, not the merge itself: that’s the only place this code can leak attempt-authored files back into the project root. I’m checking whether the existing test scaffolding can force that sync path to fail so the new operator-attention case is proven rather than assumed.

data.output: // to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is

<-ops.firstDiffReady

	wg.Add(1)
	go func() {
		defer wg.Done()
		results[1], errs[1] = landLocked(r.dir, reqs[1], ops)
	}()

	wg.Wait()

	for i, err := range errs {
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("land %d: %v", i, err)
		}
		if results[i] == nil || results[i].Status != "landed" {
			t.Fatalf("land %d: expected landed result, got %+v", i, results[i])
		}
	}

	if status := r.runGit("status", "--porcelain"); status != "" {
		t.Fatalf("main checkout dirty after concurrent land sync:\n%s", status)
	}
}

type interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps struct {
	real RealLandingGitOps

	mu             sync.Mutex
	syncCalls      int
	firstDiffReady chan struct{}
	firstSyncDone  chan struct{}
}

var _ LandingGitOps = (*interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps)(nil)

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error) {
	return g.real.CurrentBranch(dir)
}

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error) {
	return g.real.ResolveRef(dir, ref)
}

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error {
	return g.real.UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA)
}

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
	g.mu.Lock()
	g.syncCalls++
	callNum := g.syncCalls
	g.mu.Unlock()

	if callNum == 1 {
		defer close(g.firstSyncDone)

		runGit := func(args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
			cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", dir}, args...)...)
			return cmd.CombinedOutput()
		}

		out, err := runGit("read-tree", "HEAD")
		if err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
		}
		diffOut, diffErr := runGit("diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD")
		if diffErr != nil {
			return nil
		}
		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
		if g.firstDiffReady != nil {
			close(g.firstDiffReady)
		}
		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
		if len(changed) == 0 {
			return nil
		}

		var indexFiles []string
		var removedFiles []string
		for _, f := range changed {
			probe := exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
			if probe.Run() == nil {
				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
			} else {
				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
			}
		}

		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
			for _, f := range indexFiles {
				if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(filepath.Join(dir, f)), 0o755); err != nil {
					return fmt.Errorf("creating checkout parent for %s: %w", f, err)
				}
			}
			out2, err2 := runGit(append([]string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}, indexFiles...)...)
			if err2 != nil {
				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
			}
		}

		for _, f := range removedFiles {
			_ = os.Remove(filepath.Join(dir, f))
		}
		return nil
	}

	select {
	case <-g.firstSyncDone:
		return g.real.SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev)
	default:
		return nil
	}
}

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error {
	return g.real.AddWorktree(dir, path, rev)
}

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) AddBranchWorktree(dir, path, branch string) error {
	return g.real.AddBranchWorktree(dir, path, branch)
}

func (g *interleavedCheckoutSyncLandingGitOps) RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error {
	return g.real.RemoveWorktree(dir, path)

Observed on eitri 2026-07-06: after execute-bead attempt 20260706T043710-ff946509 (bead pqueue-68aa6cfd, project /Users/erik/Projects/pqueue), six modified source files (crates/pqueue-core/src/domain.rs, crates/pqueue-core/tests/core_domain_tests.rs, crates/pqueue-engine/src/command.rs, crates/pqueue-engine/src/compose.rs, crates/pqueue-resp/tests/e2e.rs, +1) were left uncommitted in the PROJECT ROOT working tree — not in the attempt's isolated worktree. The attempt's implementation commit was correctly preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/20260706T043710-ff946509/0, but the leaked working-tree copy (which DIFFERED from the preserved commit) tripped the pre-claim dirty-root gate, and the pqueue worker exited with operator-attention on every relaunch from ~04:37Z until an operator stashed the files at ~14:35Z. Zero pqueue beads executed in that window.

# ROOT CAUSE (to verify)
ddx try/work attempts execute in isolated worktrees; nothing should write source files to the project root. The likely writer is the land/merge path: a land attempt (merge of the candidate into the project-root checkout) that failed post-merge left conflict-resolution or checkout state behind without aborting/resetting. Investigate the land path for the outcome recorded for attempt 20260706T043710 (tracker commits 9e0545a/e8344a0 in pqueue show its evidence landed but the source commit did not), and any early-exit between 'git merge/checkout' and 'reset --hard on failure'.

# PROPOSED FIX
Make the land path transactional on the project-root checkout: on any land failure after mutating the working tree, restore the pre-land state (git merge --abort / git reset --hard to the pre-land rev — permitted here because the attempt's work is already preserved in the iteration ref; never touch operator-authored dirty paths that predate the land). Assert post-land that the project root is clean of attempt-authored paths, and emit operator_attention with the preserved-ref name if restoration fails.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dirty-root gate semantics (correct; it did its job).
- Relaunch-suppression cadence (ddx-f5bc9fc5).

Related: ddx-f8fae018 (landing-index jam sibling), ddx-f5bc9fc5.

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestLand_RestoresProjectRootOnFailedLand proves: a land that fails mid-merge leaves the project-root working tree exactly as before the land (no attempt-authored files dirty), with the attempt preserved in its iteration ref.

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.

- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.

- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.

Observed on eitri 2026-07-06: after execute-bead attempt 20260706T043710-ff946509 (bead pqueue-68aa6cfd, project /Users/erik/Projects/pqueue), six modified source files (crates/pqueue-core/src/domain.rs, crates/pqueue-core/tests/core_domain_tests.rs, crates/pqueue-engine/src/command.rs, crates/pqueue-engine/src/compose.rs, crates/pqueue-resp/tests/e2e.rs, +1) were left uncommitted in the PROJECT ROOT working tree — not in the attempt's isolated worktree. The attempt's implementation commit was correctly preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/20260706T043710-ff946509/0, but the leaked working-tree copy (which DIFFERED from the preserved commit) tripped the pre-claim dirty-root gate, and the pqueue worker exited with operator-attention on every relaunch from ~04:37Z until an operator stashed the files at ~14:35Z. Zero pqueue beads executed in that window.

# ROOT CAUSE (to verify)
ddx try/work attempts execute in isolated worktrees; nothing should write source files to the project root. The likely writer is the land/merge path: a land attempt (merge of the candidate into the project-root checkout) that failed post-merge left conflict-resolution or checkout state behind without aborting/resetting. Investigate the land path for the outcome recorded for attempt 20260706T043710 (tracker commits 9e0545a/e8344a0 in pqueue show its evidence landed but the source commit did not), and any early-exit between 'git merge/checkout' and 'reset --hard on failure'.

# PROPOSED FIX
Make the land path transactional on the project-root checkout: on any land failure after mutating the working tree, restore the pre-land state (git merge --abort / git reset --hard to the pre-land rev — permitted here because the attempt's work is already preserved in the iteration ref; never touch operator-authored dirty paths that predate the land). Assert post-land that the project root is clean of attempt-authored paths, and emit operator_attention with the preserved-ref name if restoration fails.

# NON-SCOPE

)

for pkg in $packages; do
  [ -d "$pkg" ] || continue
  if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"; then
      status=1
    fi
  fi
done

exit "$status"
